resources.string: Grab the screen from anywhere, crop it, and share it — without saving anything to your device.

Choose Circle to Share as the digital assistant app (Default digital assistant app → Circle to Share). This makes the assistant gesture — press and hold the power button, or swipe up from a bottom corner — open the crop overlay. If a \"Use screenshot\" toggle is shown there, enable it.

If your device doesn\'t hand screenshots to third-party assistants, the accessibility service captures the screen instead. Under Accessibility → Circle to Share, turn on the main \"Use\" toggle. (This also powers the floating button below.)
Invoke your assistant (hold the power button, or swipe up from a bottom corner), circle what you want, then hit Share. Tip: the test button below captures this screen.

Ways to trigger a capture — use any of them:

• Power button: press and hold (works via the assistant role from step 1). Set under System → Gestures → Press & hold power button → Digital assistant, if it opens the power menu instead.

• Corner swipe: swipe up from a bottom corner — another way to invoke the assistant from step 1, if enabled in your system settings.

• Floating button (below): the app's own always-on-screen bubble you can tap from any app. Drag it anywhere; it stays out of the way. Needs the accessibility fallback (step 2) enabled.
A small draggable bubble on screen. Tap it to capture, crop, and share from anywhere.
Enable the accessibility fallback (step 2) first — the bubble runs on it.
Drag to crop — or share the full screen
Circle what you want — or share the full screen
Draw a freeform loop around what you want and its outline becomes the crop box. Turn off to drag a rectangle directly.
Couldn\'t get a screenshot. Enable the fallback in the Circle to Share app, or turn on \"Use screenshot\" in assistant settings.
Set Circle to Share as your assistant app first.
Couldn\'t open settings on this device.

Lets Circle to Share capture the screen when you trigger it — with the assistant gesture or the app\'s floating button — so you can crop and share it. Nothing is captured until you trigger it, and nothing is saved to your device.
